chunk_summary: Management of growth faltering in infants under six months focuses on breastfeeding assessment, maternal health, and structured dyad care to address feeding issues and prevent stunting.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of breastfeeding assessment in managing growth faltering in infants under six months.
Identify maternal factors such as anemia, infection, or depression that impact infant feeding capacity.
Learn facility-based care protocols for infants with danger signs or severe malnutrition.
Develop structured feeding plans and follow-up strategies to support exclusive breastfeeding.
key_facts: Growth faltering in infants under six months is managed as a mother–infant dyad problem.
Skilled breastfeeding assessment is prioritized over formula escalation.
Anthropometry, such as weight-for-age, is used to identify at-risk infants in primary care.
Danger signs like dehydration, hypothermia, or persistent vomiting require facility-based care.
The MAA (Mothers’ Absolute Affection) program provides breastfeeding support in India.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Link families to breastfeeding support systems where available, including the national MAA (Mothers’ Absolute Affection) program framework.
document_key_topics: Management of growth faltering in infants under six months
